description: KV cache redundancy in transformer inference: proof that keys/values are deterministic projections of the residual stream, bit-identical recomputation, and the KV-Direct memory-efficient inference system. From 'The Residual Stream Is All You Need' (arXiv:2603.19664).
source: Qasim, Zhang, Shaheen, Alharith, Zhang — arXiv:2603.19664, 2026. Pack extracted 2026-07-06 from primary source.

triples.subject: KV cache
triples.object: The transformer KV cache is mathematically redundant: keys and values at every layer can be deterministically recomputed from the residual stream with zero reconstruction error — bit-identically, not approximately.

triples.subject: keys and values
triples.object: Deterministic linear projections of the residual stream at each layer. Caching them stores derived quantities; the residual stream is the sufficient statistic for attention over past tokens.

triples.subject: residual stream KV paper
triples.object: Verified across six models from 135M to 4B parameters spanning four architecture families, with perfect output matching between cached and recomputed inference.

triples.subject: KV-Direct
triples.object: A memory-efficient inference method that checkpoints only residual stream vectors (~5 KB per token) instead of full KV pairs (~136 KB per token), recomputing keys/values on demand — a ~27x per-token memory reduction.

triples.subject: KV-Direct
triples.object: Over 20 conversation turns, peak memory holds at 42 MB versus 103 MB for standard KV caching, while maintaining 100% token accuracy. Existing cache-eviction baselines degrade to 5-28% token accuracy under comparable memory budgets.

triples.subject: KV recomputation
triples.object: Recomputing keys/values from the residual stream can be up to 5x faster than reading cached tensors from memory at moderate batch sizes — compute can beat memory bandwidth.

triples.subject: KV cache eviction methods
triples.object: Eviction/compression baselines accept lossy approximation (5-28% token accuracy retention in the paper's long-conversation tests); KV-Direct is lossless because it stores the quantity keys/values are derived from rather than approximating the derivatives.

triples.subject: residual stream
triples.object: Elevated from an interpretability construct to the canonical state object of transformer inference: it is the minimal information that must persist per token, with attention KV as disposable derived views.

triples.subject: residual stream KV paper
triples.object: Long-context and multi-turn inference on memory-constrained hardware becomes feasible by trading modest recompute for ~27x smaller per-token state; also implies KV-cache-injection techniques can equivalently operate on residual stream vectors.
